insights.text: 📊 Your dataset contains 21 rows and 7 columns. After cleaning, 20 rows were retained (95.2% of the original data).
🔧 Columns 'Age', 'Score' had moderate missing data (10–30%). Numeric columns were filled with the median value, and text columns with the most frequent value.
🗑️ Found and removed 1 duplicate rows (4.8% of your dataset). Duplicate records can skew analysis results, so this cleanup improves accuracy.
↔️ A moderate positive correlation (0.64) was found between 'Salary' and 'Score'. As 'Salary' increases, 'Score' tends to increase as well.
〰️ A weak weak correlation (-0.18) was found between 'Age' and 'Salary'. These two columns don't show a strong linear relationship.
〰️ A weak weak correlation (0.17) was found between 'Age' and 'Score'. These two columns don't show a strong linear relationship.
📈 Column 'Salary' failed the Shapiro-Wilk normality test and is right-skewed (positively skewed). The mean (85650.0) is higher than the median (76500.0), suggesting extreme values pull the average.
📊 Based on the Shapiro-Wilk statistical test, columns 'Age', 'Score' follow an approximately normal distribution, which is ideal for many statistical analyses and machine learning models.

🎯 Mutual Information analysis shows 'Age' shares the most non-linear information (normalized score: 1.000) with the dataset's highest variance column, followed by 'Score' (score: 0.000). These columns contain the most unique signal.
🏷️ Column 'Name' has 20 unique categories. The most common value is 'Alice', appearing in 5.0% of records (1 times).
🏷️ Column 'Department' has 5 unique categories. The most common value is 'Engineering', appearing in 35.0% of records (7 times).
🟢 Overall Data Quality Score: 85/100 — Excellent. Your dataset is in great shape for analysis.
